project.name: TUGAS-AKHIR-VAR-TATA-SURYA
project.currentFocus: Verify mobile AR scanner after reverting harmful body-lock layout, switching video preview to portrait-fill, and shrinking the GLB Sun node.

lastAchievements: Phase 0 baseline: git worktree was clean before edits and npm run build passed before the cleanup patch.
Phase 1 cleanup: AR viewport sync now runs only while scanner is active, preventing late AR.js layout sync from affecting the landing page.
Phase 1 cleanup: scanner close now schedules repeated post-close cleanup passes to remove late AR.js video/canvas artifacts.
Phase 1 layout: landing/app/base view heights no longer depend on scanner --vh; scanner keeps the dynamic viewport behavior only while AR is active.
Reverted the Phase 1b body-lock approach after real-device feedback showed Chrome Android zoomed the page out into multiple tiled views.
Restored fixed full-viewport views to prevent mobile landing tiling after scanner close.
Changed AR video presentation from contain to cover and removed forced stream width/height/aspectRatio constraints so scanner visually fills portrait screens without imposing 16:9 constraints.
Detected a named Sun_7 node inside solar_system.glb and added runtime scaling for sun nodes to keep the sun from covering the phone camera view.
Set final super-micro model scale: solar root 0.010, fallback 0.055, root Y 0.025 for very small marker appearance on phones.
Adjusted planet detail scales into requested 0.008-0.010 range across all planets.
Improved tap usability while model is tiny by increasing hit-zone radii and raising minimum hit-zone wrapper scale to 0.22.
Applied portrait-first camera constraints preset (9:16, 720x1280 ideal) plus fallback landscape preset on rotation.
Kept viewport layer sync and zoom-min reset path so mobile preview stays upright and less over-zoomed.
pendingIssues: Current patch needs real-device confirmation on Infinix Note 40 because layout and camera behavior differ from desktop preview.

If the camera still feels visually zoomed, choose between object-fit cover (portrait fill) and contain (no crop but black bars); mobile AR.js cannot guarantee a true 9:16 camera stream on every browser.
Final visual calibration still requires real-device check on Infinix Note 40 and iPhone Safari.
If tap hit-zones drift after final marker scale, hitZonePosition/hitZoneRadius tuning is still required.
nextBestActions: Deploy and test on phone: close scanner should no longer show tiled/9-page landing.
Scan Hiro marker and verify the Sun is no longer full-screen while other planets remain usable.
Run full on-device checklist for AR start, switch camera, close scanner, and reopen scanner cycle.
After hit-zone tuning finalizes, set DEBUG_HIT_ZONES to false for production visual cleanliness.
Capture browser-specific notes for Android Chrome vs iOS Safari camera-switch limitations.
